policy: Use reusable sources for in-app question text only when the license or project terms allow it.
Use official SAT sources as metadata-only study logs unless written permission allows storing question text.
For public release, keep sourceName, licenseNote, and reviewStatus visible for every imported or generated question.

sources.name: OpenSAT / PineSAT
sources.licenseSummary: OpenSAT README describes the project as an open-source/free SAT question databank and says the API is free to use and modify.
sources.allowedUse: Local seed bank, subject to admin review for accuracy and originality.
sources.appPolicy: Imported directly as sourceType=opensat and reviewStatus=needs_review.

sources.name: OpenStax Exercises
sources.licenseSummary: OpenStax Exercises says many questions are Creative Commons Attribution licensed, but each question needs its own copyright/licensing details checked.
sources.allowedUse: Use as a candidate source only after item-level license review and attribution.
sources.appPolicy: For now, use OpenStax as a concept/reference source for original SAT Studio questions, not as copied text.

sources.name: OpenStax textbooks
sources.licenseSummary: OpenStax textbooks are free and Creative Commons licensed, with attribution and license terms depending on the specific title.
sources.allowedUse: Use for lessons, concept references, and original/adapted practice where attribution is preserved.
sources.appPolicy: Use as concept references for foundation math/writing explanations; do not copy textbook exercise text without title-level attribution review.

sources.name: CK-12
sources.licenseSummary: CK-12 grants a limited license for educational purposes and has restrictions on resale, commercial use, aggregation, and some redistribution.
sources.allowedUse: Private educational reference and concept mapping; review terms before public redistribution.
sources.appPolicy: Do not copy CK-12 question text into public bank. Use only as a concept reference unless permission/license is verified.

sources.name: College Board official SAT material
sources.licenseSummary: Official SAT material is owned by College Board and should not be copied into a public app without permission.
sources.allowedUse: Metadata-only official practice log: score, source, module, question number, skill, mistake note.
sources.appPolicy: Never import official prompts, passages, answer choices, or explanations into reusable question bank.

sources.name: CrackSAT.net
sources.licenseSummary: CrackSAT copyright page says users cannot reproduce any material from the website in any format.
sources.allowedUse: Reference/link only unless permission is granted. Private family study should be kept out of public bank.
sources.appPolicy: Do not scrape or copy CrackSAT prompts into public content. If a family imports material privately, mark it private_family and never expose to public accounts.

sources.name: Khan Academy / College Board SAT content
sources.licenseSummary: Khan/College Board SAT license notice says the SAT content is copyrighted and Creative Commons licenses do not apply.
sources.allowedUse: Link out or log metadata only.
sources.appPolicy: Do not copy Khan/College Board SAT question text into SAT Studio.
